content.title: Pokedex
content.description: A modern Next.js web application that allows users to explore the complete Pokémon database through an intuitive interface. The application features real-time search functionality with debounced input, infinite scrolling for seamless browsing, and detailed Pokémon information pages. It provides an engaging way to discover and learn about different Pokémon species using data from the official PokeAPI.
content.role: I built the entire Pokémon Explorer application from scratch, implementing a full-stack React solution with modern web development practices. I created the responsive UI components using Tailwind CSS, implemented infinite scroll functionality with React Query, built a custom debounced search hook, and developed dynamic routing for individual Pokémon detail pages. I also established proper TypeScript type definitions for API responses and ensured accessibility throughout the application.

achievements: Created a performant web application that handles large datasets efficiently through pagination and caching
Implemented responsive design that works seamlessly across all device sizes
Built accessible UI components following WCAG guidelines
Optimized user experience with debounced search reducing unnecessary API calls
Developed a scalable component architecture that can be easily extended
technologies: TanStack Query for data fetching and caching
React hooks and custom hook development
Intersection Observer API for infinite scrolling

screenshots: Main homepage showing the Pokémon grid layout with search functionality
Infinite scroll demonstration showing loading states
Pokémon detail page displaying comprehensive information
Search interface with real-time filtering results
